title: Automata Forge
description: A from-scratch theory-of-computation lab spanning the WHOLE Chomsky hierarchy AND the temporal logic above it. NEWEST — v8 LOGIC mode, a complete from-scratch LTL MODEL CHECKER that lifts the lab from finite words to the INFINITE traces of reactive systems. Write a Linear Temporal Logic formula and watch the GPVW TABLEAU (Gerth-Peled-Vardi-Wolper 1995 — the construction inside SPIN's ltl2ba) turn it into a generalized BÜCHI AUTOMATON by unrolling a U b ≡ b ∨ (a ∧ X(aUb)) and a R b ≡ b ∧ (a ∨ X(aRb)), then DEGENERALIZE it (the Baier-Katoen counter construction) to an ordinary Büchi automaton — the ω-word cousin of the NFA — drawn with the app's pan/zoom/export renderer (propositional guards under each state, double-ring accepts, a start arrow per initial state). Build a system as a KRIPKE STRUCTURE in a tiny DSL, then MODEL-CHECK it by the VARDI-WOLPER automata-theoretic method: form the product A(¬φ) ⊗ M, search for a reachable accepting state on a cycle, and get back either a proof the property holds or an ANIMATED LASSO COUNTEREXAMPLE (a stem leading into a forever-repeated loop, with a playhead). An ω-word tracer checks any ultimately-periodic word against both the automaton and an INDEPENDENT direct-semantics oracle, and the live Verify tab differentially proves the whole chain — across 400 random (formula, word) pairs the automaton for φ accepts a word IFF the independent LTL semantics says it satisfies φ (0 mismatches), exactly one of A(φ)/A(¬φ) accepts each word, and every counterexample is replayed as a genuine violating model path. ALSO — v7 Learn mode, a complete from-scratch implementation of ANGLUIN'S L* ACTIVE-LEARNING algorithm: the target machine is HIDDEN and the app must RECONSTRUCT its minimal DFA by asking only membership and equivalence queries — and the teacher is the lab's OWN product-automaton equivalence check, which hands back the shortest distinguishing counterexample. Watch the iconic OBSERVATION TABLE fill in cell by cell (rows coloured by signature, so same colour = same discovered state; closedness defects flagged in the boundary the instant before they are promoted), scrub through every atomic step — each membership bit, each closing and consistency repair, each conjecture and counterexample — toggle between the Angluin-1987 (add every prefix to S) and Rivest-Schapire-1993 (binary-search one distinguishing suffix into E) counterexample rules and see RS use far fewer queries, and watch the hypothesis DFA grow until it provably equals the target, with a live read-out of membership/equivalence query counts. It converges to exactly the Hopcroft-minimal DFA — differential-tested over 16 curated + 400 random targets under both strategies. ALSO — v6 Parse mode, a complete from-scratch PARSER GENERATOR built on the FIRST/FOLLOW engine: the top-down LL(1) predictive table M[A,a] (with conflict detection for left-recursion / common-prefix grammars) and the ENTIRE bottom-up tower LR(0) < SLR(1) < LALR(1) < LR(1) — the canonical item-set automaton (closure/goto, LR(1) lookahead propagation via FIRST(βa), and LALR by core-merging proven to yield exactly |LR(0)| states), drawn with the app's pan/zoom/export renderer; the ACTION/GOTO parse tables with shift-reduce & reduce-reduce conflicts flagged; a live grammar CLASSIFIER (which classes the grammar belongs to, and why the rest fail); and ANIMATED stack-driven parsers — a predictive LL(1) parser building the leftmost tree and a shift-reduce LR parser building the rightmost tree in reverse, both step-scrubbable. A curated gallery puts each grammar on a different rung (Knuth's pointer grammar is LALR(1)-not-SLR(1); the classic reduce/reduce-on-merge grammar is LR(1)-not-LALR(1); dangling-else is an unavoidable shift/reduce). Every parser is differential-tested to accept exactly L(G) against the Earley oracle. REGULAR (level 3): compile a regex into a parse tree, Thompson e-NFA, subset-construction DFA, Hopcroft-minimized DFA and a Brzozowski-derivative DFA; step strings through any of them; compare two regexes via the product automaton (union, intersection, difference, symmetric difference), decide equivalence/containment and prove inequality with the shortest distinguishing string; or draw your own automaton in Build mode, with a Myhill-Nerode table and direct NFA-to-regex. CONTEXT-FREE (level 2) — Grammar mode: analyze a grammar (nullable / generating / reachable / useless, FIRST & FOLLOW), normalize to Chomsky Normal Form step-by-step, recognize strings two ways (CYK table + Earley chart), render the derivation tree with ambiguity detection, sample the language, compile to a pushdown automaton with an animated stack, and play the CFL pumping lemma. NEW in v5 — Machine mode, the TOP of the hierarchy (level 0/1): a single-tape Turing machine simulator with an animated two-way tape, full configuration trace, a state-transition diagram and a delta-table, a linear-bounded (context-sensitive) mode, and a gallery of deciders for languages no grammar can describe — a-n b-n c-n and w#w are NOT context-free yet a TM decides them live — plus transducers, Rado's busy beaver, and a nondeterministic-TM configuration search. Bridges run both ways: every regex becomes a right-linear grammar AND a read-only Turing machine. No libraries — every parser, automaton algorithm, the graph layout and the SVG renderer are hand-written and differential-tested.
